title: Significant Problem, Public Value, and Durable Need · Decision Evidence Lens: Durability Change
summary: A novel technical idea lacks evidence of a consequential recurring problem, reachable affected people, existing alternatives, or value beyond demonstration. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone durability change increment in significance and public-value case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
Connect intervention to behavior and outcome through an explicit causal hypothesis with confounders and falsification evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Durability Change · Theory Durability Change
steps.instruction:
theory case — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Durability Change contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Durability Change · Theory Durability Change.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon. For Predict Durability Change · Theory Durability Change, keep Durability Change connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Durability Change condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines — Controls perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust product requirements and distinguishes automated checks from human conformance evaluation.

content.text: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Durability Change while producing significance and public-value case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon. A defensible solution applies Durability Change, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to significance and public-value case.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-durability-change — Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
focus: capstone-durability-change
decision-evidence: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Current demand guarantees a durable need. This fails the Durability Change contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.

steps.title: Defend the Durability Change model · Theory Durability Change
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Durability Change: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.

Evidence target: Defend the Durability Change model · Theory Durability Change.
steps.why: Retrieving Durability Change through the concrete significance and public-value case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Durability Change model · Theory Durability Change, keep Durability Change connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
